examples: The constellation Vulpecula is a veritable entire assembly line of newborn stars.
Scientists pointed Nasa's Infrared Telescope Facility at a distant planet called HD 189733b some 63 light years away in the constellation of Vulpecula.
Known by the popular name of the Dumbbell Nebula, the beautifully symmetric interstellar gas cloud is over 2.5 light-years across and about 1,200 light-years away in the constellation Vulpecula.
